note_data.title: Web Development
keyPoints: World Wide Web (WWW) is a collection of interconnected documents and resources linked by hyperlinks and URLs
HTML (HyperText Markup Language) is the standard markup language used to create web pages
Web browser is a software application used to access and view websites on the Internet
Website is a collection of related web pages containing text, images, videos or other digital assets
Homepage is the main page of a website that serves as the starting point for navigation
Hyperlink is a reference or navigation element in a document to another section or document
URL (Uniform Resource Locator) is the address used to access resources on the Internet
HTML tags are keywords enclosed in angle brackets that define elements and structure of web pages
Web server is a computer system that stores and delivers web pages to users over the Internet

definitions.definition: A collection of interconnected documents and other resources, linked by hyperlinks and URLs, accessible via the Internet using web browsers.
HyperText Markup Language is the standard markup language used to create and design web pages and web applications.
A software application used to access, retrieve and view websites, web pages and other content on the World Wide Web.
A collection of related web pages, including multimedia content, typically identified with a common domain name and published on web servers.
Uniform Resource Locator is a reference or address used to access resources on the Internet, specifying the location of a resource and the protocol used to access it.

importantQuestions.question: Define World Wide Web (WWW) and web browser.
importantQuestions.answer: WWW is a collection of interconnected documents linked by hyperlinks and URLs. Web browser is software used to access and view websites on the Internet.

importantQuestions.question: Explain the basic structure of HTML document with examples of common HTML tags.
importantQuestions.answer:
HTML document has basic structure: DOCTYPE declaration, root element, section containing metadata and section containing visible content. Common tags include

to

for headings,

for paragraphs, for links, for images,

for divisions,
for line breaks, and
    ,
      ,
    1. for lists. Each tag has opening and closing format like content.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is the standard markup language for creating web pages?
Which tag is used to create hyperlinks in HTML?

commonMistakes: Students often confuse WWW with Internet - WWW is a service that runs on Internet
Writing HTML tags without proper opening and closing brackets
Confusing URL with domain name - URL is complete address including protocol
Not memorizing exact textbook definitions for WWW, HTML and web browser
Mixing up different HTML tags and their purposes in answers
examTips: This chapter usually has 1-2 MCQs about HTML tags and web terminology in every BSEK paper
Definition questions about WWW, HTML, and web browser appear frequently
Always write complete HTML tag syntax with angle brackets in answers
Memorize the basic HTML document structure - it appears in long questions regularly
note_data.keyDiagram: Basic HTML Document Structure showing DOCTYPE declaration, html root element, head section (with title, meta tags), and body section (with headings, paragraphs, links, and other content elements) - students must draw this hierarchy clearly
